title: Remove instanced micro-geometry system

steps.reason: Remove the system implementation, its shaders, and its dedicated test suite

changes: Delete the 'microgeometry' SingleRotationEntry from singleEntries array (line 164)

steps.action: Remove crystal+microgeometry compound mode from compoundModes.ts
changes: Delete the 'crystal+microgeometry' entry from COMPOUND_MODE_DEFS array (lines 43-49)
Delete the 'microgeometry' case from primaryCountForSystem switch (line 71)
Remove maxInstances values from all three tier return objects in computeQuality (lines 75, 78, 80)
Delete the 'microgeometry' case from extractSystemConfig switch (lines 141-147)

steps.action: Update modeManager tests to remove microGeometry references
changes: Delete test T-056-38 (lines 739-749) — tests MicroGeometry as a mode entry
Remove microgeometry entries from tests T-054-49 (line 758), T-054-50 (line 780), T-054-51 (line 803) — adjust mock array sizes and mode counts accordingly

verification: npm run build — must pass with no errors
npm run test — all tests pass

notes: noise3d.glsl and chromaticDispersion.glsl are shared shader includes used by other systems — do NOT delete
The modeManager.ts itself needs no changes — it is generic and works with whatever entries are passed to it
After removal, the mode rotation drops from 8 single modes to 7, and compound modes drop from 4 to 3
maxInstances can be fully removed from QualityProfile since no other system uses it

acceptance_criteria_map.The microGeometry system (US-056) is rem: Steps 2-3: remove from singleEntries, systemRegistry, and compound modes
acceptance_criteria_map.No instanced mesh rendering of small cub: Step 1: deleting microGeometry.ts removes all InstancedMesh usage for small primitives
